observations.recommendation: 1. To create an Inventory Committee and conduct Physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type annually and reported on the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; and 2. To maintain a complete, updated and reconciled PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards by the Accounting and CGS Office respectively.

We recommended that: a. The Municipal Accountant oblige the officer concerned to prepare the required liquidation report as soon as a project is completed; b. Effect the prompt transfer for the cost of completed project from Trust Fund to the General Fund; and c. Develop an effective monitoring system on the status of the project implementation and submission of the corresponding liquidation report.
We recommended the agency to strengthen its internal control to safeguard its assets and to check the accuracy and reliability of its accounting data.

We recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to maintain and submit Subsidiary Ledger for all funds and to strictly comply with the provision of Section 12 of the MNGAS Volume II, Section 114 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 2010-001.
We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit monthly monitoring reports for all on going infrastructure projects in the Municipality.

We recommended that concerned personnel, particularly authorized drivers of government vehicles, to submit regularly to the Office of the Auditor, within ten (10) days of the following month, the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption in accordance with government rules and regulations, attached is the COA Circular 97-61 complete with sample form of monthly report of fuel consumption.

We recommended the LCE to direct the following: a) To appropriate at least 5% of the annual budget for GAD and to maximize its utilization through the implementation of GAD related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which fund were provided. b) Management to abide by the GAD annual plan and budget for the agency to achieve its objective of strengthening public information on GAD issues and policies.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor information should: a. Immediate submission of a complete list of all completed, on-going and those that are to be implemented containing all necessary information such as: i. Project Name ii. Implementing Unit/Office/Division iii. Brief description of the projects/programs/activities iv. Contractor or Supplier v. Mode of procurement vi. Funding source vii. Cost of approved budget viii. Project duration including start and completion dates ix. Location; b. Imposed strict implementation of the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 – 004; and c. Always provide the Audit Team with update information as to the status of the publicity of projects/programs/activities/ in order to allow timely validation of the same.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger Accounts for all funds as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.

We have recommended the following: a. For the GSO to conduct physical count for all PPE and prepare property card (PC) as prescribed by NGAS manual, and failure to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); b. Coordination between the GSO and the Accounting Office and ensure that their records on all PPE owned by the Municipality tally.

We recommend that: a. The Municipal Accountant oblige the officer concerned to prepare the required liquidation report as soon as a project is completed; b. Effect the prompt transfer for the cost of completed project from Trust Fund to the General Fund; c. Develop an effective monitoring system on the status of the project implementation and submission of the corresponding liquidation report.

We recommended the Municipal head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer each year, the Municipal Engineer to submit the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and the monthly monitoring report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

Recommendation:
We recommend the (a) Municipal Accountant to submit Bank Reconciliation
Statement together with the Bank Statement to support the Cash in Bank Balance; (b)
the Municipal Treasurer to present to the Auditors Office the updated Cashbook; and
(c) the Municipal Accountant to submit subsidiary ledger for cash in bank account.

Recommendation:
We recommend the (a) Municipal Treasurer to submit monthly RCD to the Municipal
Accountant for recording purposes and submit to the Auditors Office within 10 days
of the ensuing month; and (b) Municipal Treasurer s to maintain cashbook for cash in
treasury accounts to records all collections and remittances made.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF together with the
RAAF to the Audit Team not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month.

Recommendation:
We recommend the (a) Municipal Accountant to consistently record the inventories in
their respective asset accounts and recognized as expense the value of issued
inventories by requiring the MGSO to submit SSMI promptly; and (b) the MGSO to
create an inventory Team to conduct physical count of inventories and submit the
RPCI in the prescribed format and date to the audit team.

RECOMMENDATION:
We have reiterated our previous years audit recommendation to the Management as
follows (a) To conduct Physical count of property, plant, and equipment by type
annually and reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RPCPPE) and submitted to the Office of the Auditor not later than
January 31 of each year; and (b) To maintain a complete, updated and reconciled
PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards by the Accounting and CGS Office
respectively.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer to conduct reconciliation of their reports; and to direct the (b)
Municipal Engineer to submit monthly report of PPA.

Recommendation:
We recommend the (a) Accountant to submit the required DRRM utilization report on
or before the 15th day of the ensuing month; (b) MDRRMO to submit the required
annual LDRRMFIP to the Auditors Office; (c) the MSWD to submit monthly reports
on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuances of in-kind donation; and (d) the
Accountant to transfer the unexpended DRRMF to the STF under the account of Trust
Liability DRRM.

Recommendation:
We recommend Management (a) the submission of reviewed GAD annual
Accomplishment Report; (b) to consider the establishment of a GAD office to
maintain a focused, concentrated, and coordinated response in the administration,
planning, implementation, and reporting of GAD related PPAs; and (c) to submit
DILG approved GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2021.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Accountant to (a) submit immediately the subsidiary
ledger to substantiate balances in the together with the Notes to Financial Statement
to the Auditor in accordance with COA Circular; (b) to submit annual Financial
Statement on or before February 14 of each year; and (f) to facilitate the transfer of
completed PPEs to the General Fund.

Recommendation:
We recommend the Municipal Mayor to direct the (a) Municipal Budget Officer to
submit: 1) Comparison Statement of Budget and Actual Amount and 2) Statement of
Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) to the Auditors Office;
and to direct the (b) Municipal Accountant to submit 1) Statement of Cash Flow, b)
Subsidiary Ledgers; and c) Adjusting and Closing Journal Entry Vouchers,

We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC, likewise full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend further to submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA 9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and revised annual procurement plan (APP).

We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS for LGUs in taking up the Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.

We recommended management the following: a. Require the Accountable Officer to secure the correct cash book form and established the Cashbook to commence from last examination conducted, and b. Keep an updated cashbook which shall be made available to management and COA in the conduct of audit of cash and accounts of the Accountable officer.

We have recommended the LCE to direct the Accountant to submit the above-mentioned reports within the prescribed timetables so that timely post-audit of transaction could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
We recommended Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry and to reflect its related depreciation.

We recommended that the Management: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office within the prescribed period; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should: a. Caused the immediate submission of a complete list of all completed, on-going and those that are to be implemented projects/programs/activities for the year 2020 and prior years, containing all necessary information relative thereto, such as: i. Project Name ii. Implementing Unit/Office/Division iii. Brief description of the projects/programs/activities iv. Contractor or Supplier v. Mode of procurement Vi. Funding source Vii. Cost of approved budget Viii. Project duration including start and completion dates IX. Location; b. Imposed strict implementation of the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 — 004; and c. Always provide the Audit Team with update information as to the status of the publicity of projects/programs/activities/ in order to allow timely validation of the same.

We have recommended the following: a. For the GSO to conduct physical count for all PPE and prepare property card (PC) as prescribed by NGAS manual, and b. Coordination between the GSO and the Accounting Office and ensure that their records on all PPE owned by the Municipality tally.

We recommend that: a. The Municipal Accountant oblige the officer concerned to prepare the required liquidation report as soon as a project is completed; b. Effect the prompt transfer for the cost of completed project from Trust Fund to the General Fund; c. Develop an effective monitoring system on the status of the project implementation and submission of the corresponding liquidation report.

We recommended that: the agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 for auditor’s review and verification and to facilitate the immediate preparation of the necessary correction/adjusting entries for any discrepancies or errors or other reconciling items requiring corrections in order to reflect the correct balance of cash accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that at the beginning of the year, the Municipal Engineer submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and submit monthly monitoring report of the agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 — 004.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended Management to strictly comply with the guidelines of the LBC, likewise
full disclosure policy of the government must be adhered. We recommend further to
submit a written explanation on the above audit observation.

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended that the Management strictly adhere with the provision of RA 7160, RA
9184 and LBC 125 in the preparation and submission of the supplemental budget and
revised annual procurement plan (APP).

RECOMMENDATION:
We recommended management to comply with Section 3.7 of Local Budget Circular
(LBC) 125, Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 107 of MNGAS in taking up the
Bayanihan Grant and submit to the Office of the Auditor its subsidiary ledger.
We recommended the Municipal Accountant to submit the Subsidiary Schedule of General Ledger Accounts for all funds as required under Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001.

We recommende d that the Management: Require the Municipal Accountant to submit financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the Report of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements.

Recommendation:


            We recommended the head of agency shall instruct the  Accountant
to    prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis  in
      accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011  dated  October  2,  1996  for
auditor’s    immediate   review  and  verification  and  to  facilitate  the
immediate preparation of     the  necessary    correction/adjusting  entries
for any discrepancies or errors or      other  reconciling  items  requiring
corrections in order to reflect the correct  balance  of  cash  accounts  in
      the financial statements.

RECOMMENDATION:


            We recommended that at the beginning of each year, the Municipal
      Engineer  submit   the   list   of   all   on   –   going   government
      projects/programs/activities and those  that  are  to  be  implemented
      during the year and submit monthly  monitoring  PPA’s  report  of  the
      agency, as stated in COA Circular 2013 – 004.

Direct the persons responsible to settle  the  audit  suspension
      through compliance with the requirements indicated in  the  Notice  of
      Suspension (NS) and the persons liable for the audit disallowance must
      settle the same in accordance with COA  Circular  No.  2009-006  dated
      September 15, 2009, otherwise known as the Rules  and  Regulations  on
      the Settlement of Accounts and that COA Circular  No.  2012-001  dated
      June  14,  2012.  Re:  Revised  Documentary  Requirements  for  Common
      Government Transaction must be properly observed.

Observe properly COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 that prescribed the deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, and reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.

We recommended that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from January-November, 2012 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

We further recommended that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.

Recommendation:
Observe properly COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 that prescribed the deadline for the submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, and reports and supporting documents within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.
COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009, Re: Prescribing the use of the Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts provides:
Sub-section 7.2
7.2 Responsibility of the Agency Accountant
7.2.1	The Chief Accountant, Bookkeeper or other authorized official performing accounting and/or bookkeeping functions of the audited agency shall ensure that:
a).	The reports and supporting documents submitted by the accountable officers are immediately recorded in the books of accounts and submitted to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.
sub-section 7.3
7.3 Responsibility of the Auditor
The Auditor shall:
7.3.1 enforce submission of the receipt and disbursement records with all paid vouchers, official receipts, reports and supporting documents as prescribed by the Commission and the related laws, rules and regulations, and as necessary in the course of audit;

Recommendation:
Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.
Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from November – December, 2016 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.
We further recommend that the management shall re-evaluate their internal control on accounting and financial operations to prevent possible loss of government funds due to not PPSAS compliance.
We, therefore recommend that the agency’s officials and employees shall strictly adhere the pertinent section under P.D 1445 and COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the head of agency shall instruct the Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996. The monthly BRS from-December, 2015 shall be submitted immediately to the auditor for review and verification.

Direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to present reliable data on the nature, status and balances of the accounts.

Refer to the available records and files of financial transactions of the agency in order to arrive at a reliable SL balances which correspond to the amount stated in the General Ledger of the major accounts.
